summary:
The recommended test is Non-Invasive Prenatal Testing (NIPT) using cell-free fetal DNA from maternal blood due to its high accuracy; this method avoids potential complications while providing reassurance regarding genetic risks similar to previous pregnancies involving children with Down's Syndrome. Agent assured by emphasizing safety and reliability without unnecessary medical interventions based on existing knowledge of family history. \end{document} To summarize:

Non-Invasive Prenatal Testing (NIPT) should be offered to assess the risk of Down's syndrome because it provides accurate results safely via maternal blood sample analysis. Assurance focuses on minimizing invasiveness and leveraging known familial genetics data effectively. 
```
